projectDescription: Open-source Tasmota/ESPHome replacement firmware for Tuya-based IoT modules supporting 30+ chipsets with MQTT, Home Assistant, scripting, and an extensible driver system.
keyFeatures: Supports 30+ chipsets including BK7231T/N, BL602, ESP32/ESP8266, RTL8710/8720, W600/W800, LN882H, and more
MQTT integration with Home Assistant (YAML generator and auto-discovery)
Tasmota-compatible command syntax and JSON over HTTP
Extensible driver system for TuyaMCU, I2C, SPI, power metering, LED, sensors, and custom peripherals
Advanced scripting with events, repeating timers, clock events, and change handlers
RGBCW LED control with SM2135, BP5758D, WS2812B, and PWM support
LittleFS integration for custom HTML pages, scripts, and REST API hosting
OTA firmware upgrades via web interface
Windows simulator/schematic drawer for development and testing
publicApiSurface: startDriver <DriverName> — Load a driver at runtime
backlog <cmd1>;<cmd2> — Run multiple commands in sequence
OBK_DEV_NAME/<index>/set — MQTT topic to set a channel value
OBK_DEV_NAME/<index>/get — MQTT topic to read a channel value
cmnd/OBK_DEV_NAME/<command> — Execute any command via MQTT
HTTP GET /cm?cmnd=<command> — HTTP command API (Tasmota compatible)
